questions.explanation: camelCase for variables and functions. PascalCase for classes/constructors.
SCREAMING_SNAKE for magic constants like MAX_RETRIES.

questions.prompt: Preferred quote style in modern JS?
options: Pick one and be consistent (many teams use single or template literals)
questions.explanation: Consistency > preference. Most linters enforce a single choice.

questions.prompt: Which is safer against ASI (automatic semicolon insertion) bugs?
options: Always insert semicolons at end of statements
questions.explanation: Explicit semicolons avoid rare ASI gotchas. No-semicolon style is fine too — but be aware of the trap lines starting with ( [ / + -.

questions.prompt: Preferred indentation in most JS codebases?
questions.explanation: 2 spaces is the most common convention — used by Prettier, Airbnb style, and most projects.

questions.prompt: A function named `isReady` is a strong hint that it:
questions.explanation: is/has/can prefixes → boolean predicates. Follow the convention.

questions.prompt: Which is bad practice for readability?
questions.explanation: Single-letter names (i, j, x, y) OK in tight scopes; hurt elsewhere.
